_note: Verified real programs gathered via web research on 2026-07-05. 4 of 9 planned category-batches completed before the research agents hit a session limit (women's seminaries, outreach/BT yeshivot, Chabad, Sephardic yeshivot, university programs, and Masa summer still TODO). Contains cross-batch duplicates (e.g. Har Etzion, Sha'alvim, Kerem B'Yavne, HaKotel appear in both 'modern_orthodox_mens_yeshivot' and 'hesder_and_israeli_yeshivot') that MUST be de-duplicated by slug before seeding. durationType is one of TEN_DAY|SUMMER|SEMESTER|GAP_YEAR|CUSTOM.

masa_gap_year.name: Aardvark Israel
masa_gap_year.description: A customizable gap-year and semester program combining internships or volunteering, Hebrew ulpan, academic courses for college credit, and field trips, with participants living in staffed apartments in Tel Aviv or Jerusalem.
masa_gap_year.signupInstructions: Apply online via the 'Apply Now' form at aardvarkisrael.com and use the Masa Funding Calculator to estimate grants.

masa_gap_year.name: Kivunim
masa_gap_year.description: An academic gap-year program for high school graduates based in Jerusalem that combines Jewish and Middle Eastern studies (including Hebrew and Arabic) with extensive travel to up to a dozen countries, offering a transferable Hebrew College transcript.
masa_gap_year.signupInstructions: Create an account and complete the online application at kivunim.org, submitting transcripts, two recommendation letters, test scores, and two essays.

masa_gap_year.name: BINA Gap Year
masa_gap_year.organization: BINA - The Jewish Movement for Social Change
masa_gap_year.description: A 10-month secular gap-year program in which participants live communally with Israeli peers in south Tel Aviv, study pluralistic Judaism and Hebrew, and take part in grassroots social-action volunteering.
masa_gap_year.signupInstructions: Start an application through the BINA Gap Year page at bina.org.il.

masa_gap_year.name: Masa Israel Teaching Fellows (MITF)
masa_gap_year.location: Various cities (Tel Aviv, Jerusalem, Haifa, Beer Sheva, Bat Yam, Rishon LeZion, Beit She'an)
masa_gap_year.description: A 6-10 month fellowship placing Jewish young adults as English-teaching assistants in Israeli public schools, paired with Hebrew study, professional development, and a monthly stipend.
masa_gap_year.signupInstructions: Apply through the Masa Israel Teaching Fellows program page, choosing a provider/city track.

masa_gap_year.name: Career Israel
masa_gap_year.description: A 5-6 month professional internship program for Jewish young adults over 21, offering internship placements with Israeli companies, central Tel Aviv housing, Hebrew classes, and career-development seminars.
masa_gap_year.signupInstructions: Apply via the Career Israel application portal, choosing from five annual start sessions.

masa_gap_year.name: TAMID Israel Fellowship
masa_gap_year.description: A competitive 8-week summer internship program for active TAMID Group chapter members, placing fellows at Israeli companies in finance, tech, and entrepreneurship while living in Tel Aviv.
masa_gap_year.signupInstructions: Active TAMID chapter members apply through the TAMID Group fellowship page.

masa_gap_year.name: Habonim Dror Workshop
masa_gap_year.location: Kibbutz Ein HaShofet, Tel Aviv, Kibbutz Ravid, Jerusalem
masa_gap_year.description: The longest-running Israel gap-year program, an 8-month experience combining kibbutz-based collective living, volunteering with Israel's Hanoar HaOved VeHalomed youth movement, a Poland seminar, and study of Israeli society.
masa_gap_year.signupInstructions: Apply via the online application linked from hdnaisrael.org.

masa_gap_year.name: Yahel Social Change Fellowship
masa_gap_year.description: A 9-month service-learning fellowship for ages 22-32 involving ~30 hours/week of grassroots volunteering across multiple placements, Hebrew ulpan, and immersive learning about Israeli society.
masa_gap_year.cost: ~$1,000 (heavily subsidized; includes housing and monthly stipend)
masa_gap_year.signupInstructions: Apply online through the 'Become a Volunteer' form at yahelisrael.com.

masa_gap_year.name: Sar-El (Volunteers for Israel)
masa_gap_year.description: A volunteer program placing civilians (17+) on IDF bases for 1-3 weeks to perform non-combat logistical support such as packing medical supplies and equipment maintenance, living alongside soldiers.
masa_gap_year.cost: Registration/program fee varies; participants cover own flights
masa_gap_year.signupInstructions: Complete the online application on sar-el.org at least 6 weeks before the program start.

masa_gap_year.name: Achvat Amim
masa_gap_year.description: An immersive program for young adults engaging in social-justice partnerships between Israelis and Palestinians, combining Arabic/Hebrew study, activism, and homestays.
masa_gap_year.signupInstructions: Submit a free application through achvatamim.org; a $500 deposit is due upon acceptance.

masa_gap_year.name: Reichman University Study Abroad
masa_gap_year.organization: Reichman University - Raphael Recanati International School
masa_gap_year.description: A semester or year-long visiting/study-abroad program at Israel's first private university, offering English-taught coursework across all schools.
masa_gap_year.signupInstructions: Apply through the Study Abroad section of the Raphael Recanati International School site.

modern_orthodox_mens_yeshivot.name: Yeshivat Har Etzion (The Gush)
modern_orthodox_mens_yeshivot.description: A leading Religious Zionist hesder yeshiva founded by Rav Yehuda Amital and Rav Aharon Lichtenstein, combining rigorous Talmud study with intellectual openness; its Dr. William Major Overseas Students Program integrates English-speaking students into the main beit midrash.
modern_orthodox_mens_yeshivot.signupInstructions: Apply through the yeshiva's Overseas Program office or the Joint Yeshiva Application portal.

modern_orthodox_mens_yeshivot.name: Yeshivat Sha'alvim
modern_orthodox_mens_yeshivot.description: A Religious Zionist hesder yeshiva emphasizing rigorous conceptual Talmud study and Religious Zionist values; its Moty Hornstein Institute for Overseas Students brings English-speaking students into the yeshiva alongside Israelis.
modern_orthodox_mens_yeshivot.signupInstructions: Apply via the yeshiva's overseas admissions office or the Joint Yeshiva Application portal.

modern_orthodox_mens_yeshivot.name: Yeshivat Reishit Yerushalayim
modern_orthodox_mens_yeshivot.description: A Religious Zionist gap-year yeshiva for young men focused on serious Talmud study, passionate Avodat Hashem, and character development, with a dedicated program for overseas students (in partnership with NCSY).
modern_orthodox_mens_yeshivot.signupInstructions: Apply online through the Joint Yeshiva Application portal.

modern_orthodox_mens_yeshivot.name: Yeshivat Netiv Aryeh
modern_orthodox_mens_yeshivot.description: A Religious Zionist yeshiva on the Western Wall Plaza, founded in 1967 by Rav Aryeh Bina; its One Year Program blends academic and skills-based Talmud study for overseas students, with Sephardic, British, and Shana Bet tracks.
modern_orthodox_mens_yeshivot.signupInstructions: Apply through the Joint Yeshiva Application portal or contact admissions directly.

modern_orthodox_mens_yeshivot.name: Yeshivat Hakotel
modern_orthodox_mens_yeshivot.description: A Religious Zionist yeshiva overlooking the Western Wall that integrates overseas talmidim directly with its Israeli student body while providing extra shiurim for English speakers; offers joint credit with Yeshiva University and Touro.
modern_orthodox_mens_yeshivot.signupInstructions: Apply through the Joint Yeshiva Application portal; admission is competitive due to limited overseas spots.

modern_orthodox_mens_yeshivot.name: Yeshivat Orayta
modern_orthodox_mens_yeshivot.description: A Religious Zionist, text-based yeshiva in Jerusalem's Old City for motivated post-high-school young men, offering full-day immersive learning or a flexible schedule combining Torah study with internships or coursework.
modern_orthodox_mens_yeshivot.signupInstructions: Apply online via a rolling-admissions application on Orayta's website.

modern_orthodox_mens_yeshivot.name: Yeshivat Migdal HaTorah
modern_orthodox_mens_yeshivot.description: Founded in 2012, the only gap-year yeshiva in Modi'in, built primarily for overseas students; an English-language curriculum covers Gemara, Tanach, Halacha, Jewish history and philosophy with an emphasis on real-world halacha, with students in apartments.
modern_orthodox_mens_yeshivot.signupInstructions: Apply through the Joint Yeshiva Application portal or contact Migdal HaTorah directly.

modern_orthodox_mens_yeshivot.name: Yeshivat Torat Shraga
modern_orthodox_mens_yeshivot.description: A gap-year yeshiva for North American young men founded in 1981, emphasizing rigorous independent Talmud study, intensive chavruta learning, and close rebbe-talmid relationships within a Modern Orthodox framework.
modern_orthodox_mens_yeshivot.signupInstructions: Apply through the Joint Yeshiva Application portal or contact the recruiting director.

modern_orthodox_mens_yeshivot.name: Yeshivat Lev HaTorah
modern_orthodox_mens_yeshivot.description: A Religious Zionist gap-year yeshiva for overseas young men aiming to build independent learners with devotion to Eretz Yisrael; the first Anglo yeshiva accepted into the Hesder Yeshivot organization, and runs Lev LaChayal for lone soldiers.
modern_orthodox_mens_yeshivot.signupInstructions: Apply through the Joint Yeshiva Application portal or the yeshiva's contact form.

modern_orthodox_mens_yeshivot.name: Yeshivat Ashreinu
modern_orthodox_mens_yeshivot.description: A gap-year yeshiva combining Torah study in small classes (averaging six students), individualized chessed internships, and weekly tiyulim, with strong personal mentoring in a family-like environment.
modern_orthodox_mens_yeshivot.signupInstructions: Apply through the Joint Yeshiva Application portal or contact the yeshiva directly.

modern_orthodox_mens_yeshivot.name: Yeshivat Eretz HaTzvi
modern_orthodox_mens_yeshivot.description: A Modern Orthodox, Religious Zionist gap-year yeshiva ('Serious Torah for a Complex World') emphasizing skills for independent Talmud study, contemporary halacha, and machshava, sharing a campus with Machon Lev.
modern_orthodox_mens_yeshivot.signupInstructions: Apply through the Joint Yeshiva Application portal or the yeshiva's application page.

modern_orthodox_mens_yeshivot.name: Machon Meir
modern_orthodox_mens_yeshivot.description: A Religious Zionist yeshiva inspired by Rav Kook, whose English Department combines morning Jewish studies with afternoon Hebrew ulpan, serving beginners through advanced students, including those considering aliyah.
modern_orthodox_mens_yeshivot.signupInstructions: Contact the English Department directly or apply via Masa Israel's listing.

modern_orthodox_mens_yeshivot.name: Yeshivat Kerem B'Yavneh
modern_orthodox_mens_yeshivot.description: A leading Israeli-style hesder yeshiva with a Religious Zionist outlook, drawing overseas students into a rigorous, mostly Hebrew-language beit midrash focused on independent in-depth study of Gemara and Rishonim.
modern_orthodox_mens_yeshivot.signupInstructions: Apply through the Joint Yeshiva Application portal or KBY's own online application.

modern_orthodox_mens_yeshivot.name: Yeshivat Mitzpe Yericho
modern_orthodox_mens_yeshivot.description: A Religious Zionist yeshiva founded by Rav Shabtai Sabato, shaped by the teachings of Rav Kook; it runs a dedicated overseas program for American high-school graduates in a small desert community 20 minutes from Jerusalem.
modern_orthodox_mens_yeshivot.signupInstructions: Apply through the Joint Yeshiva Application portal or contact the office directly.

hesder_and_israeli_yeshivot.name: Yeshivat Merkaz HaRav
hesder_and_israeli_yeshivot.description: Flagship religious-Zionist yeshiva gevoha founded in 1924 by Rav Kook, with 600+ students; Israeli-students-only in practice with no dedicated overseas program.
hesder_and_israeli_yeshivot.cost: No tuition charged (standard for Israeli yeshivot)
hesder_and_israeli_yeshivot.signupInstructions: Contact the yeshiva office directly; typically requires a personal interview or rabbinic referral.

hesder_and_israeli_yeshivot.name: Ponevezh Yeshiva
hesder_and_israeli_yeshivot.description: One of the world's foremost Litvish (non-Hasidic Haredi) yeshivot, re-established in Bnei Brak in 1944 with thousands of students; Israeli/Haredi-students-only with no formal overseas program.
hesder_and_israeli_yeshivot.signupInstructions: Contact the yeshiva directly; admission is highly selective, typically via rabbinic referral.

hesder_and_israeli_yeshivot.name: Mir Yeshiva (Yeshivas Mir Yerushalayim)
hesder_and_israeli_yeshivot.description: One of the largest yeshivot in the world with thousands of students; its core beit midrash is predominantly Israeli/Haredi, but it also hosts a very large, long-established American/overseas division for English speakers.
hesder_and_israeli_yeshivot.signupInstructions: Apply through the online application portal or contact the yeshiva office directly.

hesder_and_israeli_yeshivot.name: Yeshivas Brisk
hesder_and_israeli_yeshivot.description: An elite, highly selective Litvish yeshiva led by the Soloveitchik dynasty, known for the analytical 'Brisker method'; serves Israeli/Haredi students almost exclusively with no dedicated overseas program.
hesder_and_israeli_yeshivot.signupInstructions: Contact the yeshiva directly; admission is highly selective, typically via personal connection or rabbinic referral.

hesder_and_israeli_yeshivot.name: Chevron Yeshiva (Knesset Yisrael)
hesder_and_israeli_yeshivot.description: A major Litvish yeshiva with ~1,400 students, one of the most prestigious Haredi yeshivot in the world; predominantly Israeli-student focused with no formal overseas program.
hesder_and_israeli_yeshivot.signupInstructions: Contact the yeshiva office directly; admission typically requires a rabbinic referral.

hesder_and_israeli_yeshivot.name: Yeshivat Ateret Yerushalayim
hesder_and_israeli_yeshivot.description: A religious-Zionist Hesder yeshiva in the Muslim Quarter led by Rav Shlomo Aviner, ~250 students tied to the Ateret Cohanim organization; Israeli-student focused with no widely-advertised overseas program.
hesder_and_israeli_yeshivot.signupInstructions: Contact the yeshiva office directly; typically requires a personal interview.

hesder_and_israeli_yeshivot.name: Yeshivat Hesder Sderot
hesder_and_israeli_yeshivot.description: Founded in 1995 by Rabbi Dovid Fendel, now the largest Hesder yeshiva in Israel with 800+ Israeli students; no dedicated formal overseas learning track, though a US nonprofit supports it.
hesder_and_israeli_yeshivot.signupInstructions: Contact the yeshiva office directly; typically requires a personal interview.

hesder_and_israeli_yeshivot.name: Yeshivat Otniel
hesder_and_israeli_yeshivot.description: A Hesder yeshiva (~350 students) known for a Chassidic-inflected, artistic approach to religious-Zionist learning; runs a dedicated English Speakers Program with immersion, mentorship, and dedicated classes.
hesder_and_israeli_yeshivot.durationText: Hesder track spans several years; overseas program often 1 year+
hesder_and_israeli_yeshivot.signupInstructions: Apply via the English Speakers Program or contact the office directly.

hesder_and_israeli_yeshivot.name: Yeshivat Birkat Moshe (Ma'ale Adumim)
hesder_and_israeli_yeshivot.description: A premier Hesder yeshiva founded in 1977 by Rav Haim Sabato and Rav Yitzchak Sheilat (~300 students); runs a dedicated program for overseas students with its own rabbi, director, and madrichim.
hesder_and_israeli_yeshivot.durationText: Hesder track spans several years; overseas program often 1 year+
hesder_and_israeli_yeshivot.signupInstructions: Contact the yeshiva office or overseas program directly to apply.

hesder_and_israeli_yeshivot.name: Yeshivat Nir Kiryat Arba
hesder_and_israeli_yeshivot.description: A Hesder yeshiva founded in 1972 by Rabbi Eliezer Waldman as a pioneer institution of the renewed Hebron community; primarily serves Israeli students with no confirmed dedicated overseas program.
hesder_and_israeli_yeshivot.signupInstructions: Contact the yeshiva office directly; typically requires a personal interview.

hesder_and_israeli_yeshivot.name: Yeshivat Beit El
hesder_and_israeli_yeshivot.description: A religious-Zionist Hesder yeshiva founded in 1977 by students of Mercaz HaRav, long led by Rav Zalman Baruch Melamed (250+ students); maintains a modest overseas program, less prominent than at peer Hesder yeshivot.
hesder_and_israeli_yeshivot.signupInstructions: Contact the yeshiva office directly to inquire about admission or the overseas program.

hesder_and_israeli_yeshivot.name: Yeshivat Kol Torah
hesder_and_israeli_yeshivot.description: A historic Litvish yeshiva founded in 1939, the first mainstream Haredi yeshiva to teach in Hebrew rather than Yiddish, long associated with Rav Shlomo Zalman Auerbach; Israeli/Haredi students only, no overseas program.
hesder_and_israeli_yeshivot.signupInstructions: Contact the yeshiva office directly; admission typically requires a rabbinic referral.

teen_summer.name: The Anne Samson Jerusalem Journey (TJJ)
teen_summer.location: Multiple cities (Golan, Jerusalem, Tiberias, Dead Sea, Eilat)
teen_summer.description: A three-week touring experience in Israel for public-school Jewish teens, combining hikes, historical/religious sites, and Jewish heritage programming.
teen_summer.signupInstructions: Apply online through the NCSY Summer CampInTouch portal.

teen_summer.name: NCSY GIVE (Girls Israel Volunteer Experience)
teen_summer.description: A five-week volunteering and touring program for 10th-11th grade girls, featuring service work such as soup kitchens, kibbutz volunteering, and assisting children with disabilities alongside touring.
teen_summer.signupInstructions: Apply online through the NCSY Summer CampInTouch portal.

teen_summer.name: NCSY Kollel
teen_summer.description: A six-week program for 9th-12th grade boys combining intensive Torah/Talmud learning with competitive sports leagues and touring across Israel.
teen_summer.signupInstructions: Apply online through the NCSY Summer CampInTouch portal; interview required.

teen_summer.name: Yuval Yisrael (USY Israel Pilgrimage)
teen_summer.location: Multiple cities (Jerusalem, Tel Aviv, Haifa, Tiberias, Beersheba, Eilat)
teen_summer.description: A month-long immersive Israel journey for 10th-12th graders (successor to the historic USY Israel Pilgrimage), with an optional Poland add-on.
teen_summer.signupInstructions: Register online via the USY Summer Travel website; deposit due at registration.

teen_summer.name: Yallah! Israel (NFTY in Israel - Adventure)
teen_summer.description: A four-week summer program for Reform-movement teens (grades 10-12) combining touring, hiking, and social-justice engagement across Israel.
teen_summer.signupInstructions: Apply online through the URJ/NFTY in Israel program website.

teen_summer.name: Ramah Israel Seminar
teen_summer.location: Multiple cities (Jerusalem home base, plus north)
teen_summer.description: A six-week travel and study program for rising 12th-grade graduates of Ramah camps, combining hiking, text study, and discussion-based learning, with optional Sea-to-Sea Hike and Gadna tracks.
teen_summer.signupInstructions: Apply online via the Ramah Israel CampInTouch portal.

teen_summer.name: Mach Hach BaAretz
teen_summer.organization: Bnei Akiva of the US & Canada
teen_summer.description: A five-week Israel summer tour for 10th-grade graduates from Orthodox/religious-Zionist backgrounds, with historical sites, hikes, an Adventure Week track, and movement-wide events.
teen_summer.signupInstructions: Apply online through the Mach Hach BaAretz CampInTouch portal.

teen_summer.name: BBYO Passport: Israel Journey
teen_summer.location: Multiple cities (Tel Aviv, Galilee, Caesarea, Sde Boker)
teen_summer.description: A 22-day BBYO Passport trip through Israel for teens in grades 8-12, visiting major cities and lesser-known sites while engaging with diverse local communities.
teen_summer.cost: Varies by year; $3,000 RootOne voucher available for 3+ week trips
teen_summer.signupInstructions: Register online through the BBYO Summer/Passport website.

teen_summer.name: BBYO Passport: Israel Trek
teen_summer.description: A 28-day BBYO Passport journey across Israel including hiking Mount Arbel and Masada, rafting the Jordan River, Bedouin-style desert camping, and a multi-day Gadna army-style program.
teen_summer.cost: Varies by year; $3,000 RootOne voucher available for 3+ week trips
teen_summer.signupInstructions: Register online through the BBYO Summer/Passport website.

teen_summer.name: Diller Teen Fellows Israel Summer Seminar
teen_summer.organization: Diller Teen Fellows (Helen Diller Family Foundation)
teen_summer.description: The three-week Israel component of the year-long Diller Teen Fellows leadership program, bringing ~700 Jewish teens from 32 partner communities across 7 countries together for the Global Diller Congress.
teen_summer.signupInstructions: Apply through your local Diller Teen Fellows community program (year-long fellowship application required).

teen_summer.name: Young Judaea Gesher Israel
teen_summer.description: A 3.5-week pluralist Israel summer trip for teens entering 10th or 11th grade from any camp or denomination background.
teen_summer.signupInstructions: Register online through the Young Judaea Gesher application page.

teen_summer.name: Young Judaea Horizon Israel
teen_summer.description: A six-week immersive Israel adventure for Jewish teens entering 11th or 12th grade, exploring the country's history, culture, and people; eligible for RootOne and Masa grants.
teen_summer.signupInstructions: Register online through the Young Judaea Horizon Israel program page.

teen_summer.name: Machaneh Bonim B'Israel (MBI)
teen_summer.description: A four-week traveling Israel program bringing together teens from six Habonim Dror North America camps, emphasizing values-based education, hiking, and engagement with contemporary Israeli society.
teen_summer.signupInstructions: Register online through the HDNA Israel program website.

teen_summer.name: CTeen Summer: Heritage Quest (Poland-Israel)
teen_summer.description: A 22-day summer trip for teens in grades 9-12 combining a Holocaust-heritage journey through Poland with touring and volunteering in Israel, in separate boys' and girls' sessions.
teen_summer.signupInstructions: Register online through the CTeen Summer website.
